front: Artist: presented his early collages including 'I Was a Rich Man's Plaything' at the lecture 'Bunk!' at the ICA
Artist: co-founding member of the Independent Group at the ICA, alongside Richard Hamilton
Print: series depicting Ludwig Wittgenstein; one print shows him 'admiring' Betty Hutton and Betty Grable, with a text noting he ate pork pies in the front row of the cinema
Sculpture: bronze work outside the British Library with bolts joining Newton's limbs to his body; inspired by William Blake's monotype
Collage: features Mickey Mouse and a can of lemon juice in front of a woman in a gold swimsuit
